verdict.answer_first: A states the core answer immediately: "your local machine has something the deploy environment doesn't"; B starts with a likely-cause list: "Almost always one of these".
verdict.decisiveness: B is more decisive because it gives a fast triage rule: "relative path... #1 or #4" and "package name... #2 or #3"; A surveys more possibilities.
verdict.fabrication: Both make unsupported likelihood claims; A: "roughly in order of how often they bite"; B: "single most common cause". B is the stronger unproven claim.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it maps the error shape directly to next checks: "relative path" vs "package name"; A has useful commands but more breadth.
